description: Evaluate incident analysis, playbook, SLO proposal, and capacity plan quality

scenarios.query: Write a blameless postmortem for a P1 database outage. Our primary PostgreSQL database ran out of connections at 2:47 AM UTC causing complete service unavailability for 47 minutes. The database connection pool was exhausted by a batch job that opened connections without releasing them. 100% of users were affected. The on-call engineer was paged at 2:52 AM and identified the issue by 3:05 AM. Service was restored at 3:34 AM by killing the batch job and restarting the connection pool.

context.users_affected: all (12,400 active at time of incident)

expected_behaviors: Creates detailed timeline with at least 7 timestamped events
Identifies root cause as connection leak in batch job, not the batch job itself
Applies Five Whys to get past surface cause
Maps contributing factors using Swiss Cheese model
Quantifies impact in users and revenue
Includes MTTD and MTTR metrics
Proposes at least 4 action items with owners and due dates
Uses blameless language throughout — no 'should have' or 'failed to'
Includes detection analysis (why was detection delayed 5 minutes)
References prior learnings if available
quality_gates: Timeline has >= 5 events with ISO timestamps
root_cause.category is set to a valid category
action_items >= 3 with owner and due_date on each
No blame words detected (fault, blame, failed to, should have)
detection block present with method and MTTD
response_metrics block present with MTTD and MTTR

scenarios.query: Create an operational playbook for rolling back a failed production deployment. Our deployment pipeline uses GitHub Actions to build Docker images, push to ECR, and deploy to EKS via ArgoCD. Rollbacks should target the previous known-good image tag. The playbook should cover both automatic rollback (ArgoCD health check failure) and manual rollback (engineer-initiated after observing degraded metrics).

context.rollback_mechanism: ArgoCD revision rollback or manual kubectl set image

expected_behaviors: Includes both automatic and manual rollback procedures
Steps are executable by someone at 3 AM with no prior context
Includes verification steps after rollback
Covers multi-region rollback coordination
Has escalation path with time thresholds
Includes communication templates for internal and external stakeholders
Defines when to escalate from rollback to full incident response
References specific monitoring dashboards and alert names
quality_gates: escalation block present with contact chain

scenarios.query: Propose SLOs for our core REST API service. The API handles user authentication, CRUD operations for the main product entities, and webhook delivery. Current performance: p50 latency 45ms, p99 latency 280ms, error rate 0.08%, availability 99.95% over the past 90 days. Our enterprise customers expect high reliability but we have no formal SLOs today. We have 3 enterprise customers with SLA requirements and 200+ self-serve customers.
expected_behaviors: Defines SLIs for availability, latency, and error rate
Sets SLO targets based on current performance with headroom
Does not over-engineer (no 99.99% unless customer requires it)
Includes error budget calculation in concrete units
Defines fast-burn and slow-burn alerting rules
Includes error budget policy with deploy freeze criteria
Separates read and write latency SLIs if appropriate
Considers webhook delivery as a separate SLI (delivery rate, latency)
quality_gates: validate_ops.py --type slo returns exit code 0 (when validator is extended)
slos >= 3 with target, window, and rationale
error_budget_policy present with thresholds and actions
alerting_rules present with fast_burn and slow_burn
Targets are achievable (not 99.99% on a service currently at 99.95%)

scenarios.query: Create a capacity plan for Q3. We are launching a major new feature (real-time collaboration) that will increase WebSocket connections by an estimated 3-5x. Current infrastructure handles 10,000 concurrent WebSocket connections. The feature launches July 15. We also expect organic growth of 15% per quarter based on the past 4 quarters.

current_infrastructure.database: RDS PostgreSQL r6g.2xlarge, 60% CPU at peak

context.budget_constraint: Infrastructure budget increase limited to 40% for Q3

expected_behaviors: Calculates projected load combining organic growth and feature multiplier
Identifies WebSocket server capacity as the primary bottleneck
Proposes scaling strategy with cost estimates
Includes load test plan with specific scenarios
Considers database and Redis capacity alongside WebSocket servers
Addresses the budget constraint and proposes alternatives if over budget
Includes timeline with milestones before July 15 launch
Has a contingency plan if actual multiplier exceeds 5x
quality_gates: validate_ops.py --type capacity returns exit code 0 (when validator is extended)
services[] present with current_load, peak_load, headroom_percentage
growth_forecast present with methodology and projected_load
load_test_plan present with target_load and success_criteria
recommendations present with timeline and cost_estimate

common_failures: Incident reports that name individuals instead of systems
Playbooks with steps that assume context the reader doesn't have
SLO proposals that copy-paste 99.99% without justification
Capacity plans without load test criteria
No connection to organizational learning (prior incidents not referenced)
Action items without owners or due dates
No error budget policy despite proposing SLOs

key_improvements: Swiss Cheese model applied to every incident
SLO targets justified by customer need, not vanity
Error budget policy with concrete deploy-freeze criteria
Capacity plans include load test and growth forecast
All action items have named owners and due dates
Prior learnings incorporated into new artifacts
